pages.raw_text:
The plasterers' union at its meet
ing Thursday night voted to stand
by the building laborers or hodcar-
riers in their strike. This means that
the plasterers will refuse to work
upon buildings where non-union help
ers are employed.


Concerning arbitrating the differ
ences existing between the hodcar-
riers and master builders, Secretary
E. S. Potts of the trades council said
today:


We are always ready to arbitrate
all matters I'm dispute, providing the
method of choosing and the number
of arbitrators are satisfactory. How
ever. we are not seeking arbitration.
in that line must come from
the other side.


Il understand indirectly that the
master builders are anxious to arbi-
trate and have appointed a commit
tee for that purpose. but we have re-
ceived no official word from them. l
think the strike will soon be settled. -
